name: Atari 2600
notes: MOS 6507 CPU; first major home cartridge-based console

name: Nintendo Entertainment System
notes: Ricoh 2A03 CPU; revived the video game industry after the 1983 crash

name: Super Nintendo Entertainment System
notes: Ricoh 5A22, 16-bit, Mode 7 graphics

name: Sega Genesis (Mega Drive)
notes: Motorola 68000 CPU, 16-bit era

name: Game Boy
notes: Sharp LR35902 CPU; monochrome display; one of the best-selling handhelds of all time

name: Neo Geo AES
notes: Motorola 68000 + Zilog Z80; arcade-identical hardware; premium home console

name: PlayStation
notes: MIPS R3000A CPU; CD-ROM based; popularized 3D gaming

name: Nintendo 64
notes: MIPS R4300i 64-bit, cartridge-based

name: Dreamcast
notes: Hitachi SH-4 CPU, first built-in modem

name: PlayStation 2
notes: Emotion Engine CPU; DVD support; best-selling console of all time (~155 million units)

name: Nintendo GameCube
notes: IBM PowerPC Gekko CPU; miniDVD proprietary disc format

name: Xbox
notes: Intel Pentium III CPU; first Microsoft console; built-in hard drive

name: Game Boy Advance
notes: ARM7TDMI 32-bit, 240×160 display

name: Arcade
notes: Coin-operated public gaming machines; drove early game industry from 1970s–1990s

name: PlayStation 3
notes: Cell Broadband Engine CPU, Blu-ray

name: Wii
notes: IBM PowerPC Broadway, motion controllers

name: Xbox 360
notes: IBM PowerPC Xenon CPU; popularized digital game distribution on consoles

name: PlayStation Portable
notes: MIPS R4000 CPU, UMD disc format

name: Nintendo DS
notes: Dual screen, touchscreen, wireless

name: iOS
notes: Apple iPhone/iPad gaming platform; App Store launched 2008

name: Android
notes: Open-source mobile platform; Google Play Store; largest installed gaming base globally

name: PlayStation 4
notes: x86-64 AMD Jaguar, 8 GB GDDR5

name: Xbox One
notes: AMD x86-64, HDMI passthrough

name: Wii U
notes: IBM PowerPC Espresso CPU; dual-screen gameplay with GamePad controller

name: PlayStation Vita
notes: ARM Cortex-A9 CPU; dual analog sticks; OLED display on launch model

name: Nintendo 3DS
notes: Autostereoscopic 3D, dual screens

name: Nintendo Switch
notes: NVIDIA Tegra X1, handheld/docked hybrid

name: PlayStation 5
notes: AMD Zen 2 CPU, RDNA 2 GPU, custom NVMe SSD

name: Xbox Series X
notes: AMD Zen 2 CPU, RDNA 2 GPU, 1 TB NVMe SSD

name: Steam Deck
notes: AMD Van Gogh APU, SteamOS, runs PC games

name: Nintendo Switch 2
notes: NVIDIA custom SoC, 4K docked, 1080p handheld

name: PC
notes: Open platform; Windows, Linux, macOS
